fingerprint: λ_A = c/H₀ = 4,283 Mpc is the Hubble distance, a standard cosmological quantity derived entirely from globe-based measurements
globe_value: Hubble distance D_H = c/H₀ = 299,792 / 70.0 = 4,283 Mpc (standard cosmology, first measured by Hubble 1929)
match_precision: Exact — dome uses the same formula and the same numerical value, relabeled
why_it_matters: The dome's entire distance-redshift framework (z = D/λ_A) is algebraically identical to Hubble's law (z = H₀D/c). The 'prediction' reduces to plugging known distances and redshifts into a 97-year-old formula and claiming agreement as dome validation. Three specific globe dependencies: (1) H₀ = 70.0 km/s/Mpc is measured using Cepheid distances calibrated via Gaia geometric parallax on a sphere; (2) the five 'test' galaxy distances (Virgo, Fornax, NGC 5584, Coma, Perseus) are themselves measured using Cepheids and SBF anchored to LMC geometric parallax; (3) the dome has no ontological framework for where objects at 10-100 Mpc exist within a 20,015 km radius disc. The dome provides no derivation of why its aetheric refractive index gradient would produce z = D/λ_A rather than any other functional form — it simply adopts the observed relationship and relabels the mechanism.
suggested_text: The dome's λ_A = c/H₀ = 4,283 Mpc is the Hubble distance — a standard cosmological quantity first measured in 1929, using the same formula z = H₀D/c rewritten as z = D/(c/H₀). The numerical value H₀ = 70.0 km/s/Mpc is derived from Cepheid distance ladders anchored to geometric parallax on a spherical Earth. No dome parameter (disc radius, firmament height, aetheric constants) appears in this formula.

code_search.found_in: NOT FOUND — no script in the dome repository computes or validates the λ_A = c/H₀ = 4,283 Mpc value or the distance-redshift test cases
code_search.notes: WIN-055 was added in V51.0 as a static claim on wins.html. No monitoring, no computation, no validation script. The five test galaxy comparison values (Virgo -0.8%, Fornax +0.2%, etc.) exist only as text on the webpage with no computational backing. monitoring = 'none' confirmed by curmudgeon review.
